seo_description: Current time in Nashville, TN — live Central Time Zone clock, UTC offset, DST schedule, and time differences with major US and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Nashville right now?
faq.answer: Nashville, Tennessee, USA uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Clocks spring forward on the second Sunday in March and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Nashville shares its time zone with Chicago, Dallas, Houston, and Kansas City. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago.

faq.question: What time zone is Nashville in?
faq.answer: Nashville is in the Central Time Zone (CT), observing CST (UTC-6) from November to March and CDT (UTC-5) from March to November. Tennessee is entirely on Central Time — unlike neighboring states such as Georgia and Virginia, which are on Eastern Time. The IANA timezone identifier is America/Chicago.

faq.question: Does Nashville observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Nashville observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (becoming 3:00 AM CDT, UTC-5) and fall back on the first Sunday in November at 2:00 AM (becoming 1:00 AM CST, UTC-6). Tennessee has considered legislation to stay on standard time permanently, but as of 2026 continues to observe federal DST rules.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nashville and New York?
faq.answer: Nashville (CST/CDT) is 1 hour behind New York (EST/EDT) year-round. Both cities observe Daylight Saving Time on the same schedule, so the 1-hour gap is constant. When it is 9:00 AM in Nashville, it is 10:00 AM in New York.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nashville and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Nashville (CST, UTC-6) is 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles (PST, UTC-8) in winter, and 2 hours ahead during summer (CDT vs PDT). The gap is constant year-round because both cities observe DST on the same schedule. When it is 9:00 AM in Nashville, it is 7:00 AM in Los Angeles.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Nashville and London?
faq.answer: Nashville (CST, UTC-6) is 6 hours behind London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During US and UK Daylight Saving Time, Nashville moves to CDT (UTC-5) and London moves to BST (UTC+1), maintaining a 6-hour gap. There are brief transition windows in spring and autumn when the gap is 5 or 7 hours.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Nashville?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Nashville are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST/CDT, Monday through Friday. As a major hub for the music industry, healthcare (Vanderbilt, HCA Healthcare), and tourism, many Nashville businesses operate extended hours. Honky-tonks on Broadway are open daily from 10:00 AM until 3:00 AM. The Nashville Stock Exchange does not exist — Nashville businesses that trade on public markets use NYSE/NASDAQ hours (8:00 AM–3:30 PM CST).

faq.question: What is Nashville known for besides music?
faq.answer: Nashville is known as 'Music City' and is the capital of both Tennessee and country music. Beyond the Grand Ole Opry, Nashville is a major healthcare hub (home to HCA Healthcare and Vanderbilt University Medical Center), hosts Vanderbilt University and Tennessee State University, and is one of the fastest-growing cities in the United States. Nashville is in the Central Time Zone (UTC-6/UTC-5).
